authorities.role: freestanding target, kernel code model, disabled red zone, and System V C ABI
authorities.title: Rust x86_64-unknown-none platform support

authorities.role: x86-64 register calling convention and stack alignment
authorities.title: System V x86-64 psABI

build_profile.linked_input_private_dwarf: DWARF 5; canonical PKELF1 strips all section and debug tables

transfer.architecture_mode: x86-64 long mode at privilege level 0 with paging enabled

registers.rdi: canonical virtual address of the immutable PBP1 header
registers.rdx: little-endian u64 value of PBP1 magic bytes
registers.rsp: PBP1 initial_stack_top_virtual, nonzero canonical and 16-byte aligned before the wrapper call
registers.cr3: PBP1 page_table_root_physical with only inherited PWT/PCD low bits permitted

transfer.wrapper_stack_failure: halt without a language call because no ABI call is safe before stack validation
transfer.floating_point_state: not initialized or used by this boundary

mapping_preconditions.kernel_image: All PKELF1 loads and relative relocations are complete; final header/rodata, text, RELRO, and data/BSS permissions are r, rx, r, and rw with no writable-executable page.
mapping_preconditions.handoff: The complete PBP1 range is mapped supervisor-readable and non-executable at the RDI virtual address and remains immutable through intake.
mapping_preconditions.stack: The PBP1 bootstrap stack range is mapped supervisor-readable/writable and non-executable; RSP names its exclusive top and sufficient mapped space exists for the System V call.

framebuffer.presence_rule: The optional PBP1 framebuffer record is usable only when the complete aperture is identity-mapped at its physical base.
framebuffer.address_checks: base and inclusive end must be canonical identity virtual addresses; base and byte count must be four-byte aligned and the range must not overflow
framebuffer.cache_policy: preserve the effective firmware/GOP memory type; do not promote to write-back without a validated platform rule
framebuffer.lifetime_start: after final entry page tables are installed and before transfer
framebuffer.lifetime_end: before delegation to the graphics service
framebuffer.revocation: replace with the capability-confined graphics mapping and revoke the temporary identity mapping
framebuffer.failure_behavior: omit or ignore framebuffer output and retain serial/ring diagnostics

mapping_preconditions.firmware_services: No UEFI boot-service pointer is retained or callable after transfer.

validation_order: handoff pointer, size, canonical-range, and magic envelope
authenticated PBP1 kernel-entry profile or the exact QEMU-only development revalidation profile
runtime entry equals PBP1 kernel entry
captured handoff address, byte count, stack, CR3 root, IF, and DF equal the transfer contract
intake.failure_behavior: emit the stable panic class where a validated stack permits diagnostics, then disable interrupts and halt

early_diagnostics.formatting: bounded hexadecimal, decimal, and fixed ASCII only

serial.failure_behavior: disable the serial sink and continue with the ring

required_negative_controls: null, misaligned, oversized, overflowing, or noncanonical PBP1 envelope
null, misaligned, or noncanonical initial stack
malformed PBP1 bytes and missing kernel-entry profile state
runtime entry, entry offset, and stack continuity mismatch
framebuffer zero, unaligned, overflowing, noncanonical, or undersized range
linked ELF identification, header, program-header, segment, dynamic, relocation, manifest, and entry mutations
Rust and independent Python loaded-byte comparison
claim_boundary: This slice produces and qualifies a real freestanding PooleKernel PKELF1 product image and host-executed entry helpers; it is not the former empty fixture.
This standalone PKENTRY1 receipt qualifies image construction and host-executed helpers; the separate PKXFER1 receipt owns the QEMU-only live transfer claim.
The temporary framebuffer identity mapping is a mandatory caller precondition discovered in Cycle101; the PBP1 bytes alone do not prove that mapping or its cache policy.
This standalone qualification does not execute privileged port I/O or framebuffer writes; the separate PKXFER1 QEMU receipt executes only emulated devices.

The image halts after validated entry diagnostics and does not initialize descriptor tables, interrupts, memory management, capabilities, scheduling, IPC, services, drivers, PooleGlyph, PDC, or PooleGlass.

Single-host exact reproduction and finite hostile controls do not establish second-host reproducibility, universal correctness, target-firmware support, N6 completion, a bootable ISO, or production readiness.
No signing, key generation, tag, release, merge, firmware change, privileged probe, driver load, or physical-media write is authorized or performed.
